title: Northwell Health System
searchTermsTried: site:northwell.edu international observership IMG visiting
evidenceText: site:northwell.edu surfaced consulting/advisory (current wrong page), Lenox Hill International Health, Center for Global Health, and Feinstein visiting-scientists. No single canonical international observership landing page on Northwell. Suggest manual contact via International Services.
reviewerNotes: Current URL is genuinely wrong (consulting page). No clean replacement on northwell.edu — needs human dig or direct contact.

title: UCLA Health International Physician Observership
searchTermsTried: site:uclahealth.org international physician observership program
evidenceText: Current URL IS the canonical UCLA International Physician Observership page. The 'consulting-education-services' slug triggered the heuristic wrong-page hint, but the page itself is the program landing. Verified: '...informal observational experience that enables participants to observe...'
reviewerNotes: False positive in P96-2 classifier — flag the heuristic, not the URL.

title: Clinical Experience Programs (CEP) — IMG Rotations
searchTermsTried: "clinicalexperienceprograms.com" CEP IMG rotations review legitimate
evidenceText: Could not independently verify clinicalexperienceprograms.com legitimacy via web search. Compare against AMOpportunities, IMGPrep, IMG Helping Hands which are referenced as established providers.
reviewerNotes: Third-party USCE broker — needs manual due-diligence pass before keep/discard.

title: UC Irvine Medical Center
searchTermsTried: site:ucihealth.org international observership IMG visiting medical student
evidenceText: UCI Health 'Irvine Clinical Experience' explicitly excludes international students without SSN. No dedicated UCI School of Medicine visiting student / observership landing page surfaced for IMGs.
reviewerNotes: May not actually offer USCE relevant to IMGs; consider DISCARD if confirmed.

title: Albert Einstein College of Medicine — Research Fellowship
searchTermsTried: (doctrine reclassified — research path is real for IMGs but low-priority)
evidenceText: Albert Einstein Research Fellowship: postdoctoral research positions for MD/MBBS graduates. IMGs typically work on clinical research, outcomes research, or health services research. J1 visa sponsorship usually available through the institution. Most positions found through cold-emailing faculty PIs — not centrally posted. Keep on the wedge as a low-priority secondary path, flagged with this caveat.
reviewerNotes: Low confidence: no central program listing exists; applicants must cold-email faculty PIs directly. Surface caveat on the listing rather than discarding.

title: Abington Hospital — Jefferson Health
searchTermsTried: site:jeffersonhealth.org observership IMG international visiting student
evidenceText: Jefferson Einstein Philadelphia hosts 900+ rotations/yr and offers clinical observerships to LCME/AOA/ADA/CPME graduates. Application form PDF surfaces requirements: CV, USMLE/COMLEX, ECFMG.
reviewerNotes: Strong replacement. Application is a PDF — keep both source and application URL.

title: Advocate Christ Medical Center
searchTermsTried: site:advocatehealth.com observership IMG visiting medical student elective
evidenceText: Advocate Health Care official policy: 'does not provide opportunities for visitors as medical observers in its hospitals, clinics or physician offices'. Some elective rotations available for non-affiliated school students at certain sites.
reviewerNotes: Listing as 'observership' is misleading — no observerships are offered. Either reframe as 'electives only' or discard.

title: Advocate Christ Medical Center
searchTermsTried: (duplicate row)
evidenceText: Duplicate of the other Advocate Christ Medical Center listing. Same policy: no observerships.

title: Banner University Medical Center — Tucson
searchTermsTried: site:bannerhealth.com observership IMG visiting medical student elective
evidenceText: Banner Health Medical Student Programs page covers MS rotations across the system. Specific Tucson program is at the U of A College of Medicine — see sibling row for medicine.arizona.edu.

title: Banner University Medical Center / University of Arizona
searchTermsTried: site:medicine.arizona.edu visiting medical student observership IMG elective
evidenceText: U of A Tucson Visiting Medical Students page. Eligibility: 4th-year LCME/COCA program, BLS/ACLS, $125 fee. International students restricted unless sponsored by faculty.
reviewerNotes: Add caveat that international students need faculty sponsorship.

title: Baptist Health South Florida
searchTermsTried: site:baptisthealth.net observership IMG international visiting
evidenceText: Baptist Health South Florida — explicit International Observerships page + Visiting Physician + general Observer Program. International prioritization for Latin America / Caribbean applicants.
reviewerNotes: Cleanest match in batch — both source and application URLs are official.

title: Barnes-Jewish Hospital (WashU)
searchTermsTried: site:wustl.edu visiting medical student observership IMG elective
evidenceText: WashU MD program canonical Visiting Students page + How-to-Apply. VSLO-only application path. Note: no observerships, only MD electives. International eligible via VSLO.
reviewerNotes: WashU does not offer observerships — title field on USCEHub may need adjusting if it implies observership.

title: Baylor College of Medicine
searchTermsTried: site:bcm.edu observership international medical graduate visiting student elective
evidenceText: Baylor College of Medicine canonical Visiting Medical Student page. VSLO-only path. International applicants accepted Jan-June each cycle, F-1 visa required, TOEFL 100+. Baylor does NOT offer observership/shadowing.
reviewerNotes: Strong canonical replacement. Title may need 'observership → visiting student elective' edit.

title: Beaumont Hospital — Royal Oak
searchTermsTried: site:beaumont.org observership IMG visiting medical student
evidenceText: Only PDFs surfaced for student-observership at Beaumont/Corewell Dearborn. No clean HTML landing page found post-rebrand to Corewell. Consider linking the Royal Oak hospital page + the application PDF.
reviewerNotes: Check corewellhealth.org for a unified post-rebrand landing.

title: Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center
searchTermsTried: Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center BIDMC observership IMG visiting student
evidenceText: BIDMC Medical Education by Department index. Specialty observerships at BIDMC: Radiology (multiple sub-specialties), Anesthesiology, Interventional Pulmonology, Emergency Medicine (VSLO). Current source on USCEHub points to ECFMG.org which is a clearinghouse, not BIDMC.
reviewerNotes: Definite improvement over ECFMG.org placeholder.

title: Boston Medical Center
searchTermsTried: site:bmc.org observership IMG visiting medical student elective
evidenceText: Boston Medical Center Subsidized Visiting Elective Program (SVEP, up to $2,500 reimbursement). VSLO-driven. CAVEAT: international rotations currently CLOSED — flag the listing accordingly.
reviewerNotes: Add caveat: international rotations temporarily closed.

title: Brookdale University Hospital
searchTermsTried: Brookdale Hospital observership IMG international visiting medical graduate
evidenceText: Brookdale (Brooklyn) — current URL is ECFMG.org (clearinghouse). General web search returned no Brookdale-specific observership landing page. Recommend manual outreach.
reviewerNotes: Possibly hosted under One Brooklyn Health system — try onebrooklynhealth.org.

title: Brooklyn USCE — Clinical Rotations
searchTermsTried: "Brooklyn USCE" clinical rotations program IMG observership
evidenceText: Brooklyn USCE is a legitimate IMG-focused USCE provider founded 2017 by Dr. Ratesh Khillan (board-certified hem-onc, KingsBrook Jewish). Offers electives, externships, observerships, telemedicine rotations across NY/NJ/MI/UT/TX/CA/KY. The homepage IS the canonical landing page for a third-party USCE broker — exactly the audience USCEHub serves.
reviewerNotes: Heuristic flagged 'generic homepage' but a third-party USCE broker's homepage is appropriate.
